full_text:
- Mercury Rises 3 Districts | Rain Alert 4; Phalodi, Jaisalmer, Barmer 44°C
Rain alert and heatwave conditions in Rajasthan:Mercury rises across 3 districts, rain alert issued in 4; Phalodi, Jaisalmer and Barmer record 44°C
- Copy link
As the impact of the Western Disturbance weakens, heat conditions have started intensifying across Rajasthan. From Friday (May 8), a heatwave has set in over western districts, pushing temperatures in Jaisalmer and Barmer to around 44°C.
At the same time, several parts of the state also witnessed changing weather conditions. In the afternoon, districts such as Nagaur, Ajmer, Tonk and Sawai Madhopur saw cloud formation, along with dusty winds and light rainfall.
The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave warning for three districts namely Jaisalmer, Jodhpur and Barmer for Saturday. A yellow alert has also been issued for dust storms and light rain in and around Bharatpur, Dholpur, Karauli and Alwar.
Now see these 2 pictures...
These cities remained the hottest...
Heatwave in Jaisalmer, Phalodi, Barmer
In the past 24 hours, weather across Rajasthan remained largely dry, with intense sunshine driving up temperatures across the state.
The western districts of Jaisalmer, Phalodi and Barmer recorded the highest heat, with heatwave conditions prevailing in many areas. Phalodi saw the highest maximum temperature at 44.8°C, followed by Barmer at 44.6°C and Jaisalmer at 44°C.
Apart from western Rajasthan, several other districts also experienced strong daytime heat. Chittorgarh recorded 43.4°C, Jodhpur 42.9°C, Kota and Bikaner 42°C each, Udaipur 41.6°C, Bhilwara 41.4°C, Pali 40.8°C, Nagaur and Jalore 40.7°C, while Churu and Sri Ganganagar recorded around 40°C.
Rain in Tonk, dust storms in Ajmer and Sawai Madhopur
Amid severe heat conditions in western and southern Rajasthan, several districts including Tonk, Ajmer, Sawai Madhopur, Nagaur and Bundi witnessed a change in weather.
In Tonk, clouds gathered in the afternoon, followed by light rain in some areas along with dusty winds. Similar conditions were observed in parts of Bundi, Nagaur, Ajmer and Sawai Madhopur, where cloud cover and light dust storms provided temporary relief from the intense heat for residents.
Know - How will the weather be...
Condition of major cities in Rajasthan
Jaipur: 2 degree drop in temperature
Residents of Jaipur have got some relief from the intense heat conditions. On Friday, the city recorded a drop of around 2°C in the maximum temperature, bringing it down to 38.5°C, while the minimum temperature stood at 22.4°C.
Although sunny conditions persisted during the day, light winds across the city helped reduce humidity levels, offering some comfort to residents compared to the previous days.
Udaipur: Mercury rose 2.3 degrees in a single day
Udaipur witnessed severe heat conditions on Friday, with strong sunshine and heatwave-like conditions prevailing throughout the day.
On May 8, the city recorded a maximum temperature of 41.6°C, which is 2.3°C higher compared to 39.3°C recorded on May 7.
A rise was also seen in the minimum temperature, which increased from 22.0°C to 23.2°C. Continuous bright sunshine from the morning and hot winds during the afternoon caused significant discomfort to residents.
Ajmer: Relief from drizzle in the evening after day-long heat
Ajmer witnessed intense heat throughout Friday, with strong sunshine prevailing during the day. However, in the evening, the weather took a turn as dusty winds began to blow, followed by light drizzle in some parts of the city, bringing a drop in temperature and partial relief to residents.
According to Meteorological Department data, the maximum temperature on Friday was recorded at 40.4°C, while the minimum stood at 23.6°C. The department has also issued a warning of possible storms and rain in the coming period.
Sikar: 2 degree jump in minimum temperature
In Sikar district, after a relatively cooler first week of May, heat conditions have once again begun to intensify. On Friday, the region experienced bright sunshine throughout the day along with intermittent cloud cover and light winds.
Due to cloud movement, the daytime maximum temperature dropped by around 1.5°C, while the minimum temperature rose by about 2°C, reaching 23.9°C.
As per data from the Agricultural Research Centre in Fatehpur (Sikar), the day’s maximum temperature was recorded at 38.5°C.
Kota: Mercury reaches 42 degrees
The heatwave conditions in Kota city are steadily intensifying. On Friday, the maximum temperature in the city was recorded at 42°C.
With the minimum temperature also staying high at 25.4°C, there has been little relief even during the night hours.
Prolonged sunshine throughout the day, coupled with hot winds, kept streets largely empty as residents remained indoors and adopted measures to shield themselves from the extreme heat.
Jodhpur: Humidity persisted throughout the day
Jodhpur city witnessed intense heat and humidity on Friday. According to the Meteorological Department, the maximum temperature was recorded at 42.9°C, which is 1.8°C above the normal level.
A rise was also seen in night temperatures, with the minimum settling at 28.4°C, around 1.4°C higher than normal. Continuous sunshine since morning along with moisture in the atmosphere led to oppressive humid conditions, causing discomfort for residents throughout the day.
Alwar: Temperature drops after hailstorm
Alwar district witnessed completely clear weather on Friday. The effects of hailstorm and rainfall recorded in several parts of the district on Thursday were still noticeable.
Overall, heat intensity remained relatively low throughout the day. According to the Meteorological Department, the maximum temperature was recorded at around 38°C, which stayed below the normal level.

title: : Rajasthan Weather: Severe Heatwave in Jaisalmer-Barmer, Storm Alert for Jaipur-Kota - zoomnews.in
full_text:
Rajasthan is currently witnessing a mixed weather pattern as the influence of the Western Disturbance begins to subside across the state. Starting Friday, May 8, a heatwave spell has officially commenced in the western districts of the state. While several parts of Western Rajasthan are grappling with rising temperatures, other districts have experienced sudden weather changes including thunderstorms and rain. On Friday afternoon, districts such as Nagaur, Tonk, Ajmer, and Sawai Madhopur witnessed light rainfall accompanied by dust storms. The Meteorological Department has now issued a heatwave alert specifically for the western parts of the province.
Heatwave Warning for Western Rajasthan Districts
The weather department has issued a stern warning for heatwave conditions in the districts of Jaisalmer, Jodhpur, and Barmer. These western regions are expected to face intense heat and hot winds as the mercury continues to climb, while the heatwave phase, which began on Friday, is likely to intensify, affecting daily activities in these desert districts. Residents have been advised to take necessary precautions against the rising temperatures as the impact of the Western Disturbance fades.
Yellow Alert for Storms and Rain in Eastern Rajasthan

A yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain has been issued for several districts in Eastern Rajasthan, including Alwar, Bharatpur, Dholpur, Karauli, Jaipur, Sawai Madhopur, Tonk, Kota, and Bundi, along with their surrounding areas. These regions may witness localized storm activities and light showers.
Phalodi Records Highest Temperature; 12 Districts Cross 40°C
8°C. 6°C and Jaisalmer at 44°C. The heat remains intense across various other regions as well. Temperatures exceeded the 40°C mark in several districts, including Chittorgarh, Ajmer, Bhilwara, Udaipur, Kota, Bikaner, Jodhpur, Churu, Sri Ganganagar, Nagaur, Jalore, and Pali. In these areas, the sun remained harsh throughout the day, keeping the maximum temperatures consistently high.
The rainfall in Nagaur, Tonk, Ajmer, and Sawai Madhopur on Friday afternoon provided temporary relief from the heat. However, the Meteorological Department indicates that while the eastern districts remain under a yellow alert for storms, the western belt of Jaisalmer, Jodhpur, and Barmer will continue to face severe heatwave conditions as the dry weather persists.
